message:
feat(fwc): add zone-scoped MCP tool enforcement module (CUAL-A.4)

Zone-aware tool filtering with capability token validation, structured
error responses for zone violations, and zone registry for MCP server mode.
47 tests, clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.1","title":"[CUAL-A.1] OperationInfo-to-tool-schema codec (MCP, Claude, OpenAI formats)","description":"## Goal
Build a codec module that translates FCP OperationInfo structs into tool schemas for MCP, Claude tool-use, and OpenAI function-calling formats.

## Background
Every FCP connector exposes operations via handle_introspect() returning Vec<OperationInfo>. Each OperationInfo contains:
- id (e.g. github.create_issue), summary, description
- input_schema / output_schema (JSON Schema)
- capability, risk_level, safety_tier, idempotency
- ai_hints (when_to_use, common_mistakes, examples, related)

MCP tools need: { name, description, inputSchema }
Claude tools need: { name, description, input_schema }
OpenAI functions need: { name, description, parameters }

The mapping is nearly 1:1 but needs careful handling of:
- Namespace prefixing (fcp.github.create_issue vs github_create_issue)
- Risk/safety metadata as tool annotations or description suffixes
- ai_hints.when_to_use folded into description for AI consumption
- Rate limit info as tool metadata/annotations
- Approval requirements surfaced in description

## Deliverables
- New module: crates/fwc/src/tool_schema/mod.rs (or crates/fcp-cli/src/tool_schema/)
- Codec trait with format-specific implementations
- Format enum: MCP, ClaudeToolUse, OpenAIFunction, Generic
- Full round-trip tests: OperationInfo -> schema -> validate structure
- Property tests: any valid OperationInfo produces valid tool schema

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given any connector's Introspection, produce valid tool schemas in all 3 formats
- ai_hints.when_to_use and common_mistakes are preserved in descriptions
- Risk/safety metadata is machine-extractable from annotations
- Output is deterministic (same input -> byte-identical output)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.052212Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:45:24.845413Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T12:45:24.845396Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","cual","mcp"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.052212Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1189,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: The codec should live in a new module crates/fwc/src/tool_schema/ with separate files per format: mcp.rs, claude.rs, openai.rs, and a generic.rs for the shared trait. Key crate dependencies: serde_json (already in workspace). The MCP format is the primary target since Claude Code is our main consumer. OpenAI format differs mainly in field naming (parameters vs input_schema). The Claude tool-use format is identical to MCP except for the container structure.","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:34:19Z"},{"id":1196,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CUAL-A.1 codec complete: tool_schema module in fcp-cli with MCP/Claude/OpenAI format support. 41 tests, clippy clean. Structs: McpTool, ClaudeTool, OpenAiTool with ExportOptions. Handles safety metadata, AI hints, name sanitization, prefix stripping.","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:45:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.2","title":"[CUAL-A.2] fwc export-tools command for batch schema export","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc export-tools' command that exports tool schemas for one or all connectors in the specified format.

## Commands
- fwc export-tools --format mcp                    # All connectors, MCP format
- fwc export-tools --format claude github           # One connector, Claude format
- fwc export-tools --format openai --zone z:work    # Zone-scoped, OpenAI format
- fwc export-tools --format mcp --risk-max medium   # Filter by max risk level
- fwc export-tools --format mcp --output tools.json # Write to file

## Behavior
- Uses the A.1 codec to transform connector introspections
- Respects --zone flag to only include zone-authorized operations
- Supports --risk-max flag to filter out dangerous/critical operations
- Supports --capability filter to include only specific capability families
- TOON output by default (summary table), --json for machine consumption
- --output flag writes to file instead of stdout

## Why Agents Need This
When an agent starts a session, it needs to know what tools are available. 'fwc export-tools --format mcp --json' gives it a complete tool manifest it can register with its runtime.

## Dependencies
- Requires A.1 codec module
- Requires connector introspection (1g7z0.7 already provides this)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.261005Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T12:54:29.731270Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T12:54:29.731251Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","cual","export","mcp"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.261005Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1198,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CUAL-A.2 complete: 'fwc export-tools' command with MCP/Claude/OpenAI format support. Features: --format (mcp|claude|openai), --connector (single or all), --risk-max filter, --capability filter, --strip-prefix, --no-safety, --no-hints, --output file. 23 unit tests, 730 total fwc tests pass, clippy clean. Visible alias: 'fwc tools'.","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:54:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.3","title":"[CUAL-A.3] fwc serve-mcp: dynamic MCP server exposing connectors as tools","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc serve-mcp' that runs fwc as a live MCP server, dynamically exposing all configured connectors as MCP tools.

## Background
MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers expose tools, resources, and prompts to AI agents via a JSON-RPC transport (stdio or SSE). By running fwc as an MCP server, any MCP-compatible agent (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) can use FCP connectors as native tools without any manual configuration.

## Commands
- fwc serve-mcp                          # stdio transport (for Claude Code)
- fwc serve-mcp --transport sse --port 8080  # SSE transport
- fwc serve-mcp --zone z:work            # Only expose zone-authorized tools
- fwc serve-mcp --connectors github,slack # Only expose specific connectors

## Architecture
1. On startup: introspect all configured connectors, generate MCP tool definitions via A.1 codec
2. Register tools/list handler that returns tool schemas
3. Register tools/call handler that routes to connector invoke
4. Handle auth: verify MCP client has appropriate zone/capability tokens
5. Optionally expose resources: connector health, rate limits, operation history
6. Optionally expose prompts: how to use each connector

## Dynamic Behavior
- When connectors are added/removed/reconfigured, update tool list
- When connectors go unhealthy, mark tools as unavailable
- Rate limit enforcement: reject tool calls that would exceed limits

## Deliverables
- MCP server implementation using mcp-server-rs or equivalent
- stdio and SSE transports
- Tool registration from connector introspection
- Tool call routing to connector invoke
- Tests with mock MCP client

## Why This Is Transformative
This single feature means: install Claude Code + fwc serve-mcp, and your AI agent has native access to 85+ external service integrations. No per-tool MCP server installation, no manual configuration per service.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.476705Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:16:24.991664Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","dynamic","mcp","server"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.476705Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1190,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: For MCP server, evaluate mcp-server-rs or rmcp crates. The stdio transport is the highest priority (required for Claude Code integration). SSE transport is secondary (useful for remote access). The key challenge is handling long-running connector operations: MCP tool calls are expected to be relatively quick, but some connector operations (batch queries, streaming) may take seconds. Consider using MCP's progress reporting for long operations. Also consider: should each connector operation be a separate MCP tool, or should there be a single 'invoke' tool with connector/operation as parameters? Separate tools is better for agent discoverability but may hit MCP tool count limits.","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:34:19Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.4","title":"[CUAL-A.4] Per-zone MCP tool scoping and capability enforcement","description":"## Goal
Ensure MCP server mode respects FCP zone boundaries: agents only see/invoke tools they are authorized to use in their current zone.

## Background
FCP zones enforce security boundaries. A connector bound to z:work should not expose its tools to an agent operating in z:public. MCP has no native zone concept, so we must enforce this at the fwc MCP server layer.

## Behavior
- fwc serve-mcp --zone z:work only registers tools for connectors authorized in z:work
- If no --zone flag, derive zone from agent identity or MCP client metadata
- tool/call requests are validated against zone capability tokens before dispatch
- Unauthorized tool calls return structured MCP errors with explanation
- Zone transitions (agent moving from z:work to z:private) update available tools

## Deliverables
- Zone-aware tool registration logic in MCP server
- Capability token validation middleware for tool calls
- Structured error responses for zone violations
- Tests: same connector, different zones, different tool visibility","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.707022Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:53:16.933083Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","mcp","security","zones"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.707022Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.5","title":"[CUAL-A.5] MCP resource and prompt exposure for connector state and docs","description":"## Goal
Expose FCP connector state and documentation as MCP resources and prompts, enabling agents to inspect connector health, rate limits, schemas, and usage patterns without invoking operations.

## MCP Resources
- resource://connector/{id}/health         -> HealthSnapshot
- resource://connector/{id}/rate-limits    -> RateLimitStatus per pool
- resource://connector/{id}/operations     -> Vec<OperationInfo> summary
- resource://connector/{id}/history        -> Recent operation audit log
- resource://connectors/status             -> Cross-connector health dashboard

## MCP Prompts
- prompt://connector/{id}/how-to-use       -> Usage guide with examples
- prompt://connector/{id}/op/{op}/example  -> Specific operation example
- prompt://connector/{id}/troubleshoot     -> Troubleshooting guide

## Why Resources Matter for Agents
Agents need context before deciding which tool to call. MCP resources let them inspect state cheaply (no tool invocation side effects). Prompts provide structured guidance that helps agents use tools correctly.

## Deliverables
- Resource handlers for health, rate-limits, operations, history
- Prompt handlers for usage guides and examples
- Tests with mock MCP client reading resources and prompts","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.958602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:53:17.068375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","mcp","prompts","resources"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:25:21.958602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.10.1","title":"[CUAL-J.1] Cross-connector health aggregation dashboard","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc health' that shows health status of all configured connectors at a glance, with drill-down for unhealthy ones.

## Commands
- fwc health                               # All connectors
- fwc health github                        # One connector detail
- fwc health --unhealthy                   # Only show degraded/error
- fwc health --json                        # Machine-readable
- fwc health --watch 30s                   # Refresh every 30s

## Output
Connector   Status    Auth     Latency  Last Check  Issues
github      healthy   ok       45ms     2m ago      -
slack       healthy   ok       120ms    1m ago      -
twilio      degraded  ok       890ms    30s ago     High latency
jira        error     EXPIRED  -        5m ago      Token expired

## How It Works
1. For each configured connector, call handle_health()
2. Also check auth status from credential store (Track C)
3. Include latest latency from metrics
4. Highlight issues: auth expired, high latency, error state

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.2","title":"[CUAL-F.2] Named multi-step pipeline definitions in TOML","description":"## Goal
Define reusable multi-step pipelines as TOML files that chain N operations with mappings, conditionals, and error handling.

## Pipeline Definition Format
[pipeline]
name = 'notify-on-new-issues'
description = 'Check for new GitHub issues and post to Slack'
version = '1.0'

[[steps]]
id = 'fetch'
operation = 'github.list_issues'
input = { owner = '{{params.owner}}', repo = '{{params.repo}}', state = 'open', since = '{{params.since}}' }

[[steps]]
id = 'notify'
operation = 'slack.send_message'
input = { channel = '{{params.channel}}', text = 'New issues: {{steps.fetch.output.issues | length}}' }
depends_on = ['fetch']
condition = '{{steps.fetch.output.issues | length}} > 0'

[params]
owner = { type = 'string', required = true }
repo = { type = 'string', required = true }
channel = { type = 'string', default = '#general' }
since = { type = 'string', default = '24h' }

## Commands
- fwc pipeline run notify-on-new-issues --param owner=octocat --param repo=hello-world
- fwc pipeline validate my-pipeline.toml
- fwc pipeline dry-run my-pipeline.toml --param ...
- fwc pipeline list                        # Show available pipelines
- fwc pipeline show my-pipeline            # Show pipeline details

## Pipeline Storage
- User pipelines: ~/.fwc/pipelines/*.toml
- Project pipelines: .fwc/pipelines/*.toml
- Built-in recipes: bundled in fwc binary

## Deliverables
- Pipeline TOML parser and validator
- Step executor with dependency resolution
- Template rendering for step inputs (Handlebars)
- Parameter binding with defaults and validation
- fwc pipeline command family
- Tests: multi-step, conditional, parameterized, validation errors","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"AmberAspen","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:42.848832Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:13:55.263357Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T14:13:55.263342Z","close_reason":"Implemented named TOML pipeline planning in fwc: list/show/validate/run/dry-run, parser+validation, param binding, dependency ordering, plan rendering, discovery, and tests; verified with rch cargo check/test/clippy -p fwc","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","pipeline","toml","workflow"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:42.848832Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.3","title":"[CUAL-F.3] Pipeline conditional branching and error handling","description":"## Goal
Add conditional branching (if/then/else) and error handling (on_error) to pipeline definitions.

## Conditional Steps
[[steps]]
id = 'check-labels'
operation = 'github.get_issue'
input = { owner = '{{params.owner}}', repo = '{{params.repo}}', issue_number = '{{steps.fetch.output.issues[0].number}}' }
condition = '{{steps.fetch.output.issues | length}} > 0'

[[steps]]
id = 'escalate'
operation = 'slack.send_message'
input = { channel = '#urgent', text = 'Critical issue found\!' }
condition = '{{steps.check-labels.output.issue.labels | any("priority:critical")}}' 
depends_on = ['check-labels']

## Error Handling
[[steps]]
id = 'risky-op'
operation = 'github.create_issue'
input = { ... }
on_error = 'skip'  # skip | abort | retry(3) | fallback(step_id)

[[steps]]
id = 'fallback-notify'
operation = 'slack.send_message'
input = { text = 'Failed to create issue: {{steps.risky-op.error}}' }
trigger = 'on_error(risky-op)'

## Deliverables
- Condition evaluator (simple expression language)
- Error handling modes: skip, abort, retry with count, fallback to another step
- Error-triggered steps
- Pipeline execution trace showing which steps ran, skipped, failed
- Tests: branching, error-skip, error-retry, error-fallback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.087750Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:50:05.838683Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T14:50:05.838670Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conditionals","cual","errors","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.087750Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1245,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented in crates/fwc/src/. Tests pass, clippy clean. Committed via concurrent agent workflow.","created_at":"2026-03-09T14:50:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.4","title":"[CUAL-F.4] Pipeline dry-run with cost and risk estimation","description":"## Goal
Add pipeline dry-run that simulates execution without side effects and reports: estimated API calls, risk assessment, rate limit impact, and required capabilities.

## Commands
- fwc pipeline dry-run my-pipeline.toml --param owner=octocat
- fwc pipeline estimate my-pipeline.toml  # Cost/risk summary only

## Dry-Run Output
Pipeline: notify-on-new-issues (3 steps)
Step 1: github.list_issues     [safe]  ~1 API call, reads 'github.read' capability
Step 2: github.get_issue       [safe]  ~0-N API calls (conditional), reads 'github.read'
Step 3: slack.send_message     [risky] ~0-1 API call (conditional), requires 'slack.write'

Risk Assessment: MEDIUM (step 3 writes to external service)
Rate Limit Impact: github.read: 2/5000 remaining, slack.write: 1/100 remaining
Required Capabilities: github.read, slack.write
Required Approvals: step 3 requires policy approval

## Deliverables
- Pipeline simulator that resolves steps without executing
- API call estimator based on conditionals
- Risk aggregator across pipeline steps
- Rate limit impact calculator
- Required capability/approval summary
- Tests: various pipeline shapes, conditional estimation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberAspen","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.305512Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:39:44.628621Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T14:39:44.628583Z","close_reason":"Implemented manifest-backed pipeline dry-run/estimate summaries and verified via rch cargo check/test/clippy; cargo fmt --check still reports unrelated repo formatting drift","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","dry-run","estimation","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:28:43.305512Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.6.5","title":"[CUAL-F.5] Pre-built pipeline recipe library for common cross-connector workflows","description":"## Goal
Ship fwc with a library of pre-built pipeline recipes for common agent workflows that work across connector combinations.

## Recipe Categories

### Development Workflows
- issue-to-branch: GitHub/Jira issue -> create branch -> post link back
- pr-review-notify: new PR -> summarize changes -> notify Slack/Discord
- deploy-announce: release tag -> deploy status -> notify team
- bug-triage: Sentry alert -> create issue -> assign -> notify

### Communication Workflows
- cross-post: Slack message -> Discord + Telegram
- digest-email: aggregate Slack messages -> send Gmail digest
- meeting-notes: notion/logseq doc -> send to attendees

### Data Workflows
- backup-to-s3: list items from service -> export -> upload to S3
- sync-contacts: HubSpot contacts -> Google Contacts
- analytics-report: Mixpanel data -> format -> send to Slack

### Operations Workflows
- health-check-all: check all connectors -> report to Slack
- rotate-secrets: list expiring -> rotate -> update configs -> notify

## Commands
- fwc recipe list                          # Show all built-in recipes
- fwc recipe show issue-to-branch          # Show recipe details
- fwc recipe run issue-to-branch --param ...  # Run with params
- fwc recipe export issue-to-branch > my-recipe.toml  # Export for customization

## Deliverables
- 15-20 built-in recipes covering the above categories
- Recipe registry with metadata (category, required connectors, risk level)
- fwc recipe command family
- Recipe customization via export + edit + re-import

Ship fwc with a library of pre-built pipeline recipes for common agent workflows that work across connector combinations.

## Recipe Categories

### Development Workflows
- issue-to-branch: GitHub/Jira issue -> create branch -> post link back
- pr-review-notify: new PR -> summarize changes -> notify Slack/Discord
- deploy-announce: release tag -> deploy status -> notify team
- bug-triage: Sentry alert -> create issue -> assign -> notify

### Communication Workflows
- cross-post: Slack message -> Discord + Telegram
- digest-email: aggregate Slack messages -> send Gmail digest
- meeting-notes: notion/logseq doc -> send to attendees

### Data Workflows
- backup-to-s3: list items from service -> export -> upload to S3
- sync-contacts: HubSpot contacts -> Google Contacts
- analytics-report: Mixpanel data -> format -> send to Slack

### Operations Workflows
- health-check-all: check all connectors -> report to Slack
- rotate-secrets: list expiring -> rotate -> update configs -> notify

- fwc recipe list                          # Show all built-in recipes
- fwc recipe show issue-to-branch          # Show recipe details
- fwc recipe run issue-to-branch --param ...  # Run with params
- fwc recipe export issue-to-branch > my-recipe.toml  # Export for customization

## Deliverables
- 15-20 built-in recipes covering the above categories
- Recipe registry with metadata (category, required connectors, risk level)

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.7.1","title":"[CUAL-G.1] Map-over-inputs: apply one operation to N inputs in parallel","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc map' that applies a single operation to a list of inputs, executing in parallel with configurable concurrency.

## Commands
- fwc map github.get_issue --inputs '[{"owner":"o","repo":"r","issue_number":1},{"owner":"o","repo":"r","issue_number":2}]'
- fwc map github.get_issue --input-file issues.jsonl    # One JSON per line
- fwc map github.get_issue --input-template '{"owner":"octocat","repo":"hello-world","issue_number":{{item}}}' --items '1,2,3,4,5'
- fwc map github.get_issue ... --concurrency 5           # Max 5 parallel
- fwc map github.get_issue ... --on-error continue        # Don't abort on individual failures

## Output
NDJSON by default (one result per line, matching input order):
{"index":0,"status":"success","result":{...}}
{"index":1,"status":"error","error":{"code":"FCP_ERR_NOT_FOUND",...}}
{"index":2,"status":"success","result":{...}}

## Progress
When not piped, show progress bar: [=====>    ] 3/5 (60%) 2 ok, 1 err

## Rate Limit Awareness
- Query connector rate limit pools before starting
- Auto-throttle to stay within limits
- If rate limited mid-batch, pause and wait for quota reset

## Deliverables
- Map executor with parallel dispatch (tokio tasks)
- Input sources: inline JSON, JSONL file, template + items
- Concurrency control and rate limit integration
- Progress tracking
- Error handling modes: abort, continue, retry
- Tests: parallel execution, rate limiting, partial failures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:10.292964Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T13:51:34.633526Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T13:51:34.633510Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["batch","cual","map","parallel"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.7.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:10.292964Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1217,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.7.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: batch.rs created with BatchInputs (from_json_array, from_jsonl, from_template), BatchPlan, BatchSummary, OnError, results_to_ndjson.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.7.2","title":"[CUAL-G.2] Batch-from-file: heterogeneous JSONL operations","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc batch' that executes a file of heterogeneous operations (different connectors, different operations) with dependency ordering and parallel execution where safe.

{"id":"step2","connector":"slack","operation":"send_message","input":{"channel":"#dev","text":"Issues fetched"},"depends_on":["step1"]}
{"id":"step3","connector":"todoist","operation":"create_task","input":{"content":"Review issues"}}

## Commands
- fwc batch ops.jsonl                      # Execute batch
- fwc batch ops.jsonl --dry-run            # Validate and show execution plan
- fwc batch ops.jsonl --concurrency 3      # Max parallel operations
- fwc batch ops.jsonl --on-error abort     # Stop on first error
- fwc batch ops.jsonl --format ndjson      # One result per line

## Execution Order
1. Parse all operations and build dependency graph
2. Validate all inputs against schemas
3. Execute in topological order (independent ops run in parallel)
4. Collect results, keyed by operation ID

- Batch file parser and validator
- Dependency graph builder
- Topological parallel executor
- Result collection with per-operation status
- Tests: parallel, sequential, mixed dependencies, validation errors","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:10.533935Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:16:03.892523Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T14:16:03.892509Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["batch","cual","heterogeneous","jsonl"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.7.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:10.533935Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1225,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.7.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: batch_file.rs created with BatchFile parser, BatchOp, dependency graph validation (cycle detection via Kahn's algorithm), topological wave computation (ExecutionPlan), OpResult/OpStatus types.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8.2","title":"[CUAL-H.2] Event filtering by type, pattern, and field match","description":"## Goal
Add event filtering to 'fwc events tail' that lets agents subscribe to precisely the events they care about.

## Filter Syntax
- fwc events tail slack --type message.new          # By event type
- fwc events tail slack --pattern 'deploy*'         # Glob on event type
- fwc events tail slack --match 'data.channel=#general'  # Field match
- fwc events tail slack --match 'data.text~bug'     # Regex match on field
- fwc events tail slack --exclude-type reaction.*   # Exclude pattern

## Compound Filters
- fwc events tail slack --type message.new --match 'data.channel=#urgent'
- fwc events tail --all --type '*.error' --match 'data.severity=critical'

## How It Works
1. Parse filter expressions into a filter chain
2. Each event passes through all filters (AND logic)
3. Events that match all filters are emitted
4. Events that fail any filter are dropped silently

## Deliverables
- Filter expression parser
- Type filter (exact, glob, regex)
- Field match filter (equality, regex, contains)
- Exclude filter
- Filter chain with AND composition
- Tests: each filter type, compound filters, no-match scenarios","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:30:02.795158Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:32:55.565351Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T14:24:53.967983Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","events","filter","streaming"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:30:02.795158Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1228,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Another agent (events.rs) already implemented comprehensive event filtering with EventFilter trait, TypeFilter, FieldFilter, ExcludeFilter, ConnectorFilter, FilterChain + FilterChainBuilder.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8.3","title":"[CUAL-H.3] Event replay from checkpoint or sequence number","description":"## Goal
Add event replay capability: resume event consumption from a saved checkpoint or specific sequence number.

## Commands
- fwc events tail slack --checkpoint slack-cp.json   # Resume from checkpoint
- fwc events tail slack --from-seq 12345             # Resume from sequence
- fwc events replay slack --from 2026-03-09T14:00 --to 2026-03-09T15:00  # Time range

## Checkpoint Format
{"connector":"slack","last_seq":12345,"last_timestamp":"...","last_event_id":"..."}

## How It Works
1. On each event, update checkpoint file (atomic write)
2. On startup with --checkpoint, read last position and request replay from there
3. Connector subscribe() accepts optional start_from parameter
4. If connector does not support replay, emit warning and start from current

## Deliverables
- Checkpoint file writer (atomic, with temp file + rename)
- Checkpoint reader and subscribe-from-position logic
- Time-range replay for connectors that support it
- Fallback behavior for connectors without replay
- Tests: checkpoint save/load, replay, fallback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:30:02.978451Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T14:50:06.105485Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T14:50:06.105472Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cual","events","replay","streaming"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:30:02.978451Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1246,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.8.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented in crates/fwc/src/.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.9.1","title":"[CUAL-I.1] Rate limit status dashboard across all connectors","description":"## Goal
Add 'fwc rate-limits' that shows current rate limit usage across all connectors, enabling agents to plan operations within quota.

## Commands
- fwc rate-limits                          # All connectors
- fwc rate-limits github                   # One connector
- fwc rate-limits --json                   # Machine-readable
- fwc rate-limits --warning                # Only show near-limit pools

## Output
Connector      Pool           Used/Limit  Resets In  Status
github         core.read      4500/5000   12m        WARNING (90%)
github         search         8/30        1m         OK (27%)
slack          web.write      45/100      58m        OK (45%)
twilio         sms.send       198/200     3h         CRITICAL (99%)

## How It Works
1. For each configured connector, call rate_limits() to get RateLimitDeclarations
2. Query current usage from host rate limit tracking
3. Calculate percentage, time-to-reset, and status (OK/WARNING/CRITICAL)
4. WARNING: > 80% used. CRITICAL: > 95% used.

## Why Agents Need This
Before starting a batch operation, agents need to know how much quota remains. Without this, they risk hitting rate limits mid-batch and wasting work.

## Deliverables
- Rate limit aggregator across connectors
- Status calculator with thresholds

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qnchs.9.2","title":"[CUAL-I.2] Automatic throttling and quota-aware scheduling","description":"## Goal
Integrate rate limit awareness into all execution paths (invoke, map, batch, pipeline) so operations are automatically throttled to stay within limits.

## Behavior
- Before each operation: check remaining quota in relevant rate limit pool
- If quota sufficient: proceed immediately
- If quota low (< 10%): slow down (add delay between operations)
- If quota exhausted: wait for reset, show countdown
- If multiple pools apply: respect the most restrictive

## Integration Points
- fwc invoke: single operation, simple check
- fwc map: distribute operations across time window to stay within limits
- fwc batch: schedule operations based on combined rate limit budget
- fwc pipeline: estimate total rate limit cost before starting

## User Control
- --no-throttle: bypass auto-throttling (agent accepts rate limit errors)
- --max-wait 30s: maximum time to wait for quota reset before giving up
- --rate-limit-strategy aggressive|conservative|balanced: tuning

## Deliverables
- Throttle middleware for all execution paths
- Quota prediction and scheduling algorithm
- Wait-for-reset with countdown display
- Integration with map, batch, pipeline executors

- fwc rate-limits plan --batch ops.jsonl   # Rate limit feasibility for batch

## Forecast Output
github core.read: 500 remaining, 4500 used
Current rate: ~120 requests/hour
At current rate: exhausted in ~4.2 hours
Reset: in 12 minutes (full 5000 restored)
Recommendation: safe to proceed with 100 operations

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qtu","title":"fcp.grafana: Open-Source Observability Connector","description":"## Overview
Comprehensive observability connector for Grafana ecosystem, enabling AI agents to query metrics, analyze logs, inspect traces, and manage dashboards.

## Core Capabilities

### Metrics Querying (PromQL)
- Execute PromQL queries against Prometheus datasources
- Time range selection with relative and absolute times
- Aggregation and transformation functions
- Multi-datasource queries
- Query result formatting (tables, time series)

### Dashboard Access and Rendering
- List available dashboards by folder/tag
- Retrieve dashboard JSON definitions
- Render dashboard panels as images
- Variable interpolation for dynamic dashboards
- Export dashboards for backup/migration

### Alert Management
- List active and silenced alerts
- Query alert history and state transitions
- Create and modify alert rules
- Manage silence rules
- Acknowledge and annotate alerts

### Log Search (Loki Integration)
- LogQL query execution
- Log stream discovery
- Label-based filtering
- Pattern matching and parsing
- Log context retrieval around events

### Trace Inspection (Tempo Integration)
- Trace ID lookup and visualization
- Service map generation
- Span analysis and timing breakdown
- Cross-service correlation
- Trace-to-log linking

## Deployment Support

### Self-Hosted
- Direct API authentication
- Custom SSL/TLS configuration
- On-premise datasource access
- Air-gapped environment support

### Grafana Cloud
- Cloud API token authentication
- Stack management
- Usage metrics and billing info
- Multi-tenant organization support

## Implementation Notes
- Use Grafana HTTP API
- Support API key and OAuth authentication
- Handle rate limiting gracefully
- Cache frequently accessed dashboards

## Testing Requirements
- Mock Grafana server for unit tests
- Integration tests with test instance
- Performance tests for large queries
- Compatibility tests across Grafana versions","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:49.552569516Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T07:31:19.258883993Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T07:31:19.258883993Z","close_reason":"Duplicate/overlaps with flywheel_connectors-dcgw","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
